title: CircleCI の 401 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article generally provides accurate troubleshooting steps for 401 errors in CircleCI, correctly identifying common causes like invalid/missing tokens and environment variable issues.
It accurately distinguishes between 401, 403, and 429 HTTP status codes, which is crucial for proper debugging.
A significant factual inaccuracy exists regarding the usage of Project API Tokens with CircleCI API v2. Official documentation states that only Personal API Tokens are currently supported for API v2.
Some example error messages and JSON response structures are not verbatim matches in official documentation, though their general meaning and associated status codes are correct.
citation coverage is below the threshold
freshness score is below the threshold
one or more claims are unsupported or unverifiable
required_actions: Clarify and correct the information regarding Project API Token support for CircleCI API v2. Update the guidance to exclusively recommend Personal API Tokens for API v2 operations, or specify if it refers to API v1.
Add more direct and specific citations from official CircleCI documentation for the YAML configurations related to pipeline parameters and explicit environment variable settings.
For error message examples, either provide generalized descriptions of the error or explicitly note that specific message formats may vary across different API endpoints or versions.
Add links to official documentation or primary sources for key claims.
Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.

sources.title: CircleCI API v2 Documentation
sources.claim: CircleCI's 401 error is a status code indicating API authentication failure. The CircleCI API v2 documentation lists '401 Unauthorized error' as a response.

sources.title: An Introduction to OAuth 2 - DigitalOcean
sources.claim: When sending a request to CircleCI, the request is not authorized because the API token is invalid, missing, or expired. A 401 Unauthorized response indicates a 'Token Missing or Invalid' or that 'The access token expired'.

sources.title: Automating API security tests in CI/CD for Java applications - CircleCI
sources.claim: The 401 error can occur when calling the CircleCI API from a CI/CD pipeline, as seen in examples for automating API security tests in CI/CD with 401 Unauthorized responses.

sources.title: Troubleshooting CircleCI runner issue /api/v3/runner/claim was 401 (Unauthorized)
sources.claim: The article's error message example '401 Unauthorized' is similar to error logs found in CircleCI runner issues, which state 'response from POST /api/v3/runner/claim was 401 (Unauthorized)'.

sources.title: Troubleshoot - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: The error can occur when operating locally with the CLI, as CircleCI provides options for local validation, though 'frequently occurs' is a qualitative statement.

sources.title: API実行時に「401 Unauthorized」エラーが返却される原因はなんですか？
sources.claim: If a token is invalidated or deleted, or the originating user account is deleted, that token will return 401 for all subsequent requests because older authentication tokens are invalidated when new ones are acquired.

sources.title: Resolving OAuth Token Revocation Issues on CircleCI
sources.claim: If an OAuth token is revoked, CircleCI projects can halt, and re-authenticating the account is essential to restore functionality.

sources.title: Managing API tokens - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: Token validity can be checked on the CircleCI dashboard. Deleted tokens will not appear in the history, and you cannot view a token after creation, so a new one must be generated if lost or deleted.

sources.title: Managing API tokens - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: New API tokens can be generated via 'User settings' > 'Personal API Tokens' in the CircleCI application.

sources.title: User Settings → Personal API Tokens - CircleCI
sources.claim: New API tokens can be generated via 'User settings' > 'Personal API Tokens' in the CircleCI application.

sources.title: [Server] How to Create Personal API Token? - CircleCI Support
sources.claim: New API tokens can be generated via 'User settings' > 'Personal API Tokens' in the CircleCI application.

sources.title: n8n CircleCI Credentials 教學：6 步驟取得API Token
sources.claim: New API tokens can be generated via 'User settings' > 'Personal API Tokens' in the CircleCI application.

sources.title: Automating API security tests in CI/CD for Java applications - CircleCI
sources.claim: If the `CIRCLE_TOKEN` environment variable is not defined when calling the API from within a CircleCI job, the request will be sent without the token, resulting in a 401 error.

sources.title: Managing API tokens - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: CircleCI has two types of tokens: Personal API Tokens for user-level resources with full read/write permissions for v1 and v2 APIs, and Project API Tokens for specific project operations with scopes like Status, Read Only, and Admin (for v1 only).

sources.title: CircleCI API developer's guide
sources.claim: For CircleCI API v2, only Personal API Tokens are currently supported.

sources.title: CircleCI Pipeline のAPI 実行と外部パラメータによる処理の切り替え - Zenn
sources.claim: As of July 2025, CircleCI API v2 only supports Access Tokens (Personal API Tokens).

sources.title: Project tokens fail with 401 "You must log in first" · Issue #39 · CircleCI-Public/jira-connect-orb - GitHub
sources.claim: Project level tokens are for Read-Access only and might lead to 401 errors if used for operations requiring higher permissions.

sources.title: Managing API tokens - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: Project Tokens can be obtained from 'Project Settings' > 'API Permissions' in the CircleCI application.

sources.title: CI/CDのスクリプトを開発／テスト／不具合対応しやすくする方法を考えてみる - OpenStandia
sources.claim: The `circleci config validate .circleci/config.yml` command is part of the CircleCI CLI for local validation.

sources.title: Automating API security tests in CI/CD for Java applications - CircleCI
sources.claim: 401 (authentication failure) and 429 Too Many Requests (rate limiting) are distinct HTTP status codes.

sources.title: 404 Reasons Third-Party APIs Can't Get Their Errors Straight (And How to Fix It) | Truto Blog
sources.claim: 401 (authentication failure) and 429 Too Many Requests (rate limiting) are distinct HTTP status codes.

sources.title: An Introduction to OAuth 2 - DigitalOcean
sources.claim: 401 Unauthorized indicates a missing or invalid token, while 403 Forbidden indicates insufficient scope or permissions, even if the token is valid.

sources.title: 404 Reasons Third-Party APIs Can't Get Their Errors Straight (And How to Fix It) | Truto Blog
sources.claim: 401 Unauthorized indicates expired tokens, while 403 Forbidden is for missing permissions.

sources.title: CircleCI API developer's guide
sources.claim: The `curl -X GET https://circleci.com/api/v2/me -H "Circle-Token: <your-api-token>"` command should return 200 OK if the token itself is valid, as the `/api/v2/me` endpoint returns a JSON response on success.

sources.title: CircleCI API v2 Documentation
sources.claim: The `curl -X GET https://circleci.com/api/v2/me -H "Circle-Token: <your-api-token>"` command should return 200 OK if the token itself is valid, as the `/me` endpoint is part of the API v2 documentation and typically indicates success with a 200 response.

sources.title: CircleCI API v2 Reference
sources.claim: The official CircleCI API v2 Reference is located at https://circleci.com/docs/api/v2/.

sources.title: Resolving Issues with New Create Project API endpoint - CircleCI Support
sources.claim: If issues persist, contacting CircleCI Support is recommended, as suggested by links to the CircleCI Support Center in official documentation.

sources.title: Resolving Infinite Loading Issue on CircleCI Due to 401 Error - CircleCI Support Center
sources.claim: If issues persist, contacting CircleCI Support is recommended, as suggested by links to the CircleCI Support Center in official documentation.

sources.title: [SERVER] Resolving "401 Unauthorized" Error During CircleCI Server License Update
sources.claim: If issues persist, contacting CircleCI Support is recommended, as suggested by links to the CircleCI Support Center in official documentation.

unsupported_claims: The exact format of the API response example: `{"message": "Unauthorized", "errors": [{"message": "The token is invalid or has expired"}]}` was not found verbatim in official CircleCI API v2 documentation, though the 'Unauthorized' message for 401 is standard.

The specific YAML syntax for defining `CIRCLE_TOKEN: << pipeline.parameters.api_token >>` and `CIRCLE_API_TOKEN: << pipeline.parameters.project_token >>` as direct token definitions using pipeline parameters within the `.circleci/config.yml` was not directly sourced, although pipeline parameters are a feature of CircleCI.

The claim that 'Personal Token では呼べないエンドポイントも存在します' (There are also endpoints that cannot be called with a Personal Token) for API v2 is potentially misleading given that official documentation states Personal API Tokens are the *only* tokens supported for API v2.

The explicit instruction to set `CIRCLE_TOKEN` in `Project Settings -> Environment Variables` as the exact location for such a token specifically when distinguishing from API Tokens was not directly sourced verbatim, though environment variables are set in Project Settings.
複数のプロジェクトで同じ認証トークンを共有する場合、CircleCI のコンテキスト機能を使用すると管理が容易になります。
コンテキストは 'Organization Settings' -> 'Contexts' から新規作成し、環境変数を一元管理します。
YAMLでの`context: circleci-api-context` の使用は、コンテキストをワークフローに適用する方法を示しています。
